content.claim: OECD evidence indicates rural-urban academic achievement gaps generally disappear after accounting for socio-economic status, suggesting composition rather than school size drives performance gaps, but this finding has not been verified for Hungary specifically.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: General OECD pattern; no Hungary-specific causal test provided
Does not address whether Hungarian rural schools have different SES-adjusted staffing or resource levels
Assumes SES controls are sufficient to isolate location effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
